body:
## Decision
approve

## Differentiation thesis
Timeline support can be a strong differentiator if we keep it practical: continuity guardrails that catch contradictions early without forcing writers into heavyweight planning software.

## Competitor evidence
- Living Writer positions structure support for long-form drafting, and chronology management is a common pain adjacent to that workflow: https://livingwriter.com/features
- Campfire’s broader worldbuilding/planning ecosystem shows demand for continuity tooling, but complexity can be high for MVP-focused writers: https://www.campfirewriting.com/
- Inkitt’s core value is reader/publishing network effects, leaving room for us to own internal continuity quality tools during drafting: https://writer.inkitt.com

## Risks / trade-offs
- Overly strict conflict rules can frustrate creative drafting.
- Timeline metadata entry can feel like administrative burden.
- Poor visualization can make the feature look powerful but feel unusable.

## Recommendation
Approve. Require design to keep timeline optional and conflict messaging assistive (not punitive), with one-click jump-to-fix from warnings.

## Handoff
Assign to: Design
